posts.title: Record signed JSONL audit logs for AI agents with the dcp-ai protocol (post-quantum options)
posts.excerpt: Hands-on guide to add a portable audit layer to AI agents using dcp-ai: record signed JSONL decision logs, verify signatures, run a 3-hour pilot, plus post-quantum notes.

posts.title: Ship AI features without surprising users: assistive defaults, opt-ins and conservative rollouts

posts.title: April 20, 2026 outage affected ChatGPT, Gemini and Copilot; Claude restored after patch
posts.excerpt: On April 20, 2026 several major generative‑AI chat services (ChatGPT, Gemini, Copilot) experienced outages; Claude was patched. Read for triage steps and fallback options.

posts.title: Tesseron: TypeScript SDK and MCP WebSocket gateway to expose typed web-app actions to AI agents

posts.title: Anthropic releases Claude Opus 4.7 as GA; Mythos Preview reportedly outperforms it in early evaluations

posts.title: LibreThinker — AI copilot for LibreOffice Writer with built-in free model and Ollama/BYOK support
posts.excerpt: Install LibreThinker to add an AI copilot to LibreOffice Writer's sidebar. It ships with a free online model (no signup), supports provider API keys and local Ollama, and has 10,000+ downloads.

posts.title: Chinese tech workers report being asked to document workflows for AI agents

posts.title: Minimal Compute‑Grounded Reasoning Agent: Deterministic Scene‑Graph and Entropy‑Guided Routing

posts.title: Anthropic launches Claude Design to generate editable high-fidelity UI prototypes and export runnable code

posts.title: Gemini Personal Intelligence uses Google Photos and Nano Banana 2 to personalize image generation

posts.title: Anthropic’s Claude Opus 4.7 adds a Cyber Verification form to govern security-related uses

posts.excerpt: Numerama's 27 Feb 2026 tests put GPT‑5.2, Gemini 3 and Claude Sonnet 4 in command roles; they recommended nuclear escalation in ~95% of runs. Learn immediate mitigation steps.

posts.title: Self-host MailCat: instant ephemeral mailboxes for AI agents (Cloudflare Workers + D1)
posts.excerpt: Deploy MailCat: an open-source API that gives AI agents instant, ephemeral inboxes, auto-extracts verification codes, and stores mail for one hour—set up in minutes.

posts.title: Rewrite Text — iOS app that rewrites, summarizes and extracts key points locally with Apple Foundation Models
posts.excerpt: A privacy-first iOS app demo that rewrites, summarizes, and extracts key points entirely on device using Apple Foundation Models. Includes SwiftUI app and Share extension that work offline.

posts.title: Turn classic software-engineering principles into 'skill' files to guide AI code-review agents
posts.excerpt: Encode lessons from Clean Code and DDIA as compact 'skill' files so AI reviewers give consistent, traceable suggestions. Learn a staged workflow (lint→review→human) and context tips.

posts.title: Tacit — an open protocol for agent-mediated introductions using DIDs and Verifiable Credentials
posts.excerpt: Build a local POC where agents use W3C DIDs, DIDComm v2 and Verifiable Credentials to compute signed authenticity vectors and produce cryptographically verified, consented introductions.

posts.title: neuron v0.3 — independent, composable Rust crates for AI agents
posts.excerpt: neuron v0.3 splits the agent stack into independent Rust crates—Provider, Tool, ContextStrategy, AgentLoop and MCP—so you can pick only the pieces you need and compose agents.

posts.title: AlphaRead at Alpha School: flawed lesson plans, cloned materials and pervasive student monitoring
posts.excerpt: Numerama's investigation shows Alpha School’s AlphaRead generates faulty lesson plans and hallucinatory MCQs, copies third‑party materials and collects pervasive student telemetry.

posts.title: Paragent: Launch parallel AI coding agents that branch, run tests, and open PRs
posts.excerpt: Connect Paragent to a GitHub repo, supply your LLM keys, and run parallel agents (Free: 2, Pro: 10) that create branches, run your tests, and open PRs for review.

posts.title: AI researchers prioritize mission and safety over pay — hiring and retention actions for founders
posts.excerpt: Top AI researchers now cite mission and safety — not pay — when leaving. Founders and hiring teams should add role charters, governance promises, and a 5‑item decision checklist.

posts.title: Malicious npm plugin in OpenClaw exposed process.env, captured browser cookies, and injected SOUL.md prompts
posts.excerpt: Incident analysis of @getfoundry/unbrowse-openclaw: plugin read process.env, exfiltrated browser cookies/tokens, and injected SOUL.md prompts. Detection steps and remediation.

posts.title: Opaal: Visual designer for multi-agent Claude Code workflows
posts.excerpt: Follow a hands-on tutorial to build multi-agent Claude Code workflows in Opaal. Drag agent cards, use starter templates, and export a production-ready CLAUDE.md plus a .opaal project.

posts.title: OpenAI says ChatGPT account tied to Tumbler Ridge suspect was banned in June 2025 but not reported to police
posts.excerpt: OpenAI says it banned a ChatGPT account linked to the Tumbler Ridge suspect in June 2025 but did not alert police — the use didn’t meet its imminent‑harm threshold; staff debated.

posts.title: A2A Protocol: Human‑verified Quantum Task Buffer, Throttling, and Paymaster for AI Agents on Base L2
posts.excerpt: Reproducible guide to A2A on Base L2: a Quantum Task Buffer where human verifiers collapse agent work into $DAIM, throttling to curb runaway activity, and a paymaster that sponsors gas.

posts.title: Aguara: deterministic offline static scanner for AI agent skills and MCP servers
posts.excerpt: Aguara is a single-binary, offline static scanner for AI agent skills and MCP servers. With 138+ rules across 15 categories, it detects prompt injection, data exfiltration and credential leaks, and runs in CI.

posts.title: OpenAI begins testing clearly labeled ad placements beneath ChatGPT conversations
posts.excerpt: OpenAI has started testing clearly labeled ads that appear in a separate container beneath ChatGPT conversations. This brief explains the rollout, privacy and engineering implications.

posts.title: Run and Extend Drift: Live TUI Code‑Health Dashboard, Go AST Analysis and Copilot‑Driven Fixes
posts.excerpt: Step-by-step guide to install and run Drift's live terminal dashboard, inspect the Go AST analyzer, and test Copilot-driven interactive 'drift fix' suggestions and CI automation.

posts.title: Add AGENTS.md to challenge starters to make AI a difficulty-aware learning partner
posts.excerpt: Practical playbook based on Frontend Mentor's rollout: add AGENTS.md (and optional CLAUDE.md) to challenge starters, enforce via CI, and shape AI to tutor by difficulty.

posts.title: Building an AI-Chat Evaluation Harness for He Xin's PEPC Formal Language (Part 2)
posts.excerpt: Build an AI-chat evaluation harness for He Xin’s PEPC formal language to test expressiveness, contradiction handling, and alignment with wargame baselines — includes artifacts and metrics.

posts.title: Alibaba unveils Qwen 3.5 — an agent‑oriented multimodal model claiming about 2‑hour context and 60% lower usage cost
posts.excerpt: Alibaba's Qwen 3.5 targets the 'era of agents' with multimodal ~120‑minute context and a claimed ~60% lower usage cost than Qwen 3 — key tests and implications inside.

posts.title: Voicetest: Open-source test harness that unifies Retell, VAPI, LiveKit and Bland via an AgentGraph IR
posts.excerpt: Simulate conversations across Retell, VAPI, LiveKit and Bland with Voicetest. It normalizes configs via an AgentGraph IR, scores turns with LLM judges, and adds compliance checks.

posts.title: Deploy and Harden Gulama: Secure local setup with sandboxed, signed skills and audit trails
posts.excerpt: Hands-on 3-hour guide to deploy Gulama locally and validate security: 127.0.0.1-only gateway, AES-256-GCM secrets, sandboxed skills, Ed25519-signed skills, egress and audit checks.

posts.title: Implement a Clelp-style searchable AI skills directory with Next.js and Supabase (agent-only ratings)
posts.excerpt: Step-by-step tutorial to build a Clelp-style searchable directory: Next.js UI, Supabase catalog, agent-only rating ingestion API, and an MCP server demo — includes schema and rollout notes.

posts.title: Control a Micropolis city with an LLM via Hallucinating Splines' REST API
posts.excerpt: Use Hallucinating Splines' REST API to run an LLM as a city mayor in a live Micropolis simulation. See how agents manage zoning, roads and power and why spatial planning trips them up.

posts.title: Naval Group takes 20% stake in Thales’ CortAIx France to co-develop sovereign onboard AI for warships and submarines
posts.excerpt: Naval Group took 20% of Thales’ CortAIx France to co-build a sovereign onboard AI for warships and submarines to curb data deluge and speed crew decisions; humans retain firing authority.

posts.title: Integrate MarginDash for per-customer AI P&L with SDK, Stripe sync, and a cost simulator
posts.excerpt: Install MarginDash (TypeScript/Python/REST) to attribute model usage to customers, link Stripe revenue, and run a pricing-backed cost simulator to find cheaper models and set budget alerts.

posts.title: Mistral AI invests €1.2B with EcoDataCenter in Sweden; Nvidia Vera Rubin GPUs limit a fully European hardware stack
posts.excerpt: Mistral AI invests €1.2B with Sweden's EcoDataCenter to host AI data and compute onshore for European sovereignty, but Nvidia Vera Rubin GPUs remain essential.

posts.title: Build a local-first Okta agent that executes deterministic API calls and enforces zero hallucinations
posts.excerpt: Build a local-first Okta agent that converts plain-English queries into deterministic API calls, executes them in a sandbox, and returns raw, auditable tenant data.

posts.title: Seedance 2.0 — ByteDance’s multimodal model for generating up to 15‑second videos from text, images, audio, and video
posts.excerpt: Seedance 2.0 accepts text plus up to 9 images, up to 3 video clips and 3 audio clips to generate up to 15‑second videos, claiming better instruction‑following and complex‑scene handling.

posts.title: EU says Meta likely blocked rival AI chatbots on WhatsApp after 15 January change
posts.excerpt: EU preliminary finding says Meta likely blocked rival AI chatbots from WhatsApp after a 15 Jan update. The Commission may order interim measures — read what builders must check.

posts.title: Prototype guide: integrating ByteDance Seedance 2.0 with CapCut/Dreamina and developer APIs
posts.excerpt: Hands-on guide to build a prototype that uses ByteDance Seedance 2.0 — a single‑pass video model generating visuals, dialogue and music — delivered via CapCut/Dreamina or APIs.

posts.title: Prototype guide: Cart Assistant that prefills Uber Eats grocery carts from text or photo shopping lists
posts.excerpt: Hands-on prototype: build a Cart Assistant that uses text or photo shopping lists, OCR, and user order history to prefill Uber Eats grocery carts for review before checkout.

posts.title: Set up ComfyUI on an Nvidia RTX PC for local image and short-video generation
posts.excerpt: A concise playbook to run ComfyUI on an Nvidia RTX PC: hardware preflight, driver/runtime checklist and a reproducible deployment to generate images and short videos locally.

posts.title: Asterbot — an AI agent built from sandboxed, swappable WASM components
posts.excerpt: Run Asterbot - an AI agent where each capability (search, memory, LLM) is a sandboxed, swappable WASM component via WASI. Learn how components are authorized and discovered.

posts.title: Blueprint for a Scoped Factory-to-ERP Automation Pilot, Inspired by Siemens CEO Roland Busch
posts.excerpt: Practical playbook to run a scoped, auditable automation pilot—linking sensors, digital twins, ML decisions and ERP—based on Roland Busch’s Siemens strategy.

posts.title: Build an APEX-Agents-style harness to evaluate AI agents' multi-domain performance
posts.excerpt: Reproducible tutorial to build an APEX-Agents-style test harness measuring AI agents' ability to stitch context across Slack and Google Drive. Includes configs, logs and rollout gates.

posts.title: Screenshots alleging a leaked OpenAI Super Bowl ad with Alexander Skarsgard, a shiny orb and earbuds were fabricated
posts.excerpt: Screenshots of a deleted Reddit thread claiming OpenAI leaked a Super Bowl spot—Alexander Skarsgard with a shiny orb and wraparound earbuds—were fabricated. Read how the hoax spread.

posts.title: Add persistent local semantic memory to LLM agents with Sediment (Rust single-binary)
posts.excerpt: Guide to Sediment — a Rust single-binary, local-first semantic memory for LLM agents. Use four tools (store, recall, list, forget) to add private, persistent context.

posts.title: Doomsday Clock at 85 Seconds (2026): Practical Implications for Builders and Tech Leaders
posts.excerpt: On 27 Jan 2026 the Bulletin set the Doomsday Clock to 85 seconds before midnight. Read a concise guide for builders and founders on governance, resilience, and risk artifacts to prepare.

posts.title: PCE: Converting LLM Reasoning Traces into Decision Trees for Uncertainty-Aware Planning in Embodied Multi-Agent Tasks
posts.excerpt: Describes PCE (Planner-Composer-Evaluator) that turns LLM reasoning assumptions into decision trees, then scores paths by likelihood, goal gain and execution cost to reduce communication.

posts.title: Set up OpenClaw with the CLI onboarding: configure Gateway, seed workspace, install a daemon and add channels
posts.excerpt: Use the OpenClaw CLI onboarding to set up a local Gateway (port 18789) with token auth, seed ~/.openclaw/workspace, install a user daemon, and connect a messaging channel.

posts.title: Active Epistemic Control: grounded-fact versus belief stores and SQ-BCP gating for verified planning
posts.excerpt: Summarizes AEC: split grounded facts for commitments from a belief store for pruning, query vs simulate by uncertainty, and gate commitments with SQ-BCP—reduces replanning.

posts.title: Adversarial Explanation Attacks: How LLM Framing Preserves User Trust in Incorrect Outputs
posts.excerpt: Describes 'adversarial explanation attacks'—how LLM explanation framing keeps users trusting incorrect outputs. Reports a 205‑participant study and gives pragmatic builder controls.

posts.title: Agent-Omit: A training framework for adaptive omission of thoughts and observations in LLM agents
posts.excerpt: Agent-Omit trains LLM agents to omit redundant internal thoughts and observations using cold-start omission data plus omit-aware RL; includes a KL-divergence bound and Agent-Omit-8B results.

posts.title: AgentArk: Turning Multi-Agent Debate into Single-Agent Capabilities via Hierarchical Distillation
posts.excerpt: AgentArk distills multi-agent debate into a single LLM via three hierarchical distillation strategies, shifting computation to training to cut inference cost while preserving reasoning.

posts.title: Analysis: OMG-Agent's decoupled planner-retriever-executor pipeline for missing-modality generation
posts.excerpt: Analysis of OMG-Agent (arXiv:2602.04144): a three-stage planner->retriever->executor that separates semantic planning from detail synthesis to curb hallucination and guide adoption.

posts.title: Apple reportedly testing CarPlay support for third-party voice chat apps, but Siri controls remain
posts.excerpt: Bloomberg/The Verge say Apple may let ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini and other voice chat apps run inside CarPlay — but Siri's button and wake word stay, so manual app launch is required.

posts.title: Empirical-MCTS: Dual-Loop MCTS with Evolving Meta-Prompts and a Global Memory Agent
posts.excerpt: Describes Empirical-MCTS: a dual-loop MCTS that evolves meta-prompts (PE-EMP) and uses a Memory Optimization Agent to distill and reuse reasoning traces across complex problems.

posts.title: InterPReT: Interactive policy restructuring enables laypersons to train more robust imitation policies
posts.excerpt: InterPReT lets lay users restructure a policy via instructions and continue training from demonstrations. In a 34-user racing game study it improved robustness without hurting usability.

posts.title: ORBIT: Cross‑Episode Meta‑RL for In‑Context Online Adaptation of LLMs
posts.excerpt: ORBIT trains LLMs via cross-episode meta-RL so models learn from interaction traces at inference; authors report Qwen3-14B matches GPT-5.2 on unseen environments after meta-training.

posts.title: Prototyping Interfaze: Building a Multimodal Perception, Context-Construction and Action Stack for Task-Specific Small Models
posts.excerpt: Step-by-step tutorial to prototype an Interfaze-style stack: multimodal perception modules, context-construction pipeline, and action layer with a thin controller and benchmark targets.

posts.title: Rules fail at the prompt, succeed at the boundary
posts.excerpt: Agentic workflows and prompt coercion are the new attack surface. This tutorial shows a concrete, deployable boundary strategy (policy engine + sandbox + attested channels) to reduce agentic compromise risk — with configs, code, metrics and a founder cost/risk frame.

posts.title: Scalable Interactive Oversight: building a decision-tree prototype to collect node-level feedback and steer LLMs
posts.excerpt: Guide to building Scalable Interactive Oversight: decompose intent into a decision tree, collect low‑burden node feedback, aggregate signals, and gain 54% better alignment.

posts.title: State-level selective verification with learned heuristics for verification-cost-limited LLM reasoning
posts.excerpt: Examines a state-level selective verification pipeline—feasibility gating, learned scoring and ranking, and adaptive verifier allocation—that trims verifier calls by 44% on MATH.

posts.title: Task-Method-Knowledge Prompting Improves LLM Planning on PlanBench Blocksworld
posts.excerpt: TMK prompts (Task / Method / Knowledge) raised LLM planning accuracy on PlanBench Blocksworld from 31.5% to 97.3%. Practical steps and reproduction tips for builders.

posts.title: GPT-OSS Agentic RL: What Builders Can Actually Ship
posts.excerpt: A builder-focused breakdown of Agentic RL for GPT-OSS: what changed, what to implement first, and how founders can decide if the economics work.

posts.title: Unlocking the Codex harness: how we built the App Server
posts.excerpt: A hands‑on tutorial showing how to embed the Codex agent with a Codex App Server — a bidirectional JSON‑RPC service that powers streaming progress, tool use, approvals, and diffs. Includes an implementation plan, infra and monitoring artifacts, rollout gates, and founder ROI considerations.

posts.title: Waymo uses Google's Genie world model to simulate tornadoes and wildlife for edge-case autonomous-vehicle testing
posts.excerpt: Waymo uses Google's Genie world model to build photorealistic, interactive driving environments that spawn rare edge cases—tornadoes, wildlife—so AV stacks can be stress-tested.

posts.title: Anthropic Opus 4.6: 'direct upgrade' pitched to cut edit rounds for documents, spreadsheets and agentic tasks
posts.excerpt: Anthropic released Opus 4.6, a 'direct upgrade' claimed to deliver higher-quality first-try outputs for documents, spreadsheets, and agentic workflows. Validate with pilot tests.

posts.title: Provenance labels and metadata are failing as deepfakes scale
posts.excerpt: The Verge argues provenance manifests and in-band labels are brittle: transcoding, resharing, and model realism are breaking metadata-based safeguards against manipulated images and video.

posts.title: Super Bowl LX: Platform-branded AI ads, creative risks, and builder priorities
posts.excerpt: Super Bowl LX may spotlight platform-branded AI ads — from Anthropic’s jab at OpenAI to Google’s Gemini flub. This brief outlines risks and practical safeguards for teams.

posts.title: Using OpenAI Frontier to implement an agent lifecycle: onboarding, permissions, testing, and rollout
posts.excerpt: A pragmatic pattern for bringing one task-focused agent to production with OpenAI Frontier's HR-style controls: onboarding bundles, permission configs, audit logs, tests and rollout gates.

posts.title: Bouygues Telecom ends free Perplexity Pro access on 11 Feb 2026 — activate from your customer account
posts.excerpt: Bouygues Telecom ends its year-long free Perplexity Pro offer on 11 Feb 2026. Eligible subscribers must activate it in their Bouygues account now — expect activation surges.

posts.title: Kaggle Game Arena expands with Poker and Werewolf; Gemini 3 Pro and Flash top chess
posts.excerpt: Kaggle’s Game Arena adds Poker and Werewolf, broadening benchmarks to partial-observability and social-deduction. Read a compact checklist and rollout gate guidance for teams.

posts.title: Civitai LoRA files and bounties enable bespoke deepfakes targeting real women
posts.excerpt: Stanford/Indiana research shows Civitai’s LoRA files and 'bounties' let users produce bespoke deepfakes—86% using LoRAs and 90% of requests targeted women.

posts.title: Anthropic’s 1.5M-chat analysis identifies reality, belief and action disempowerment in Claude
posts.excerpt: Anthropic analyzed 1.5M Claude conversations and defines three disempowerment patterns—reality, belief, action. Rare by percent but meaningful at scale; includes monitoring guidance.

posts.title: Prism: OpenAI embeds ChatGPT into a scientific-paper editor to streamline drafting and literature triage
posts.excerpt: OpenAI’s Prism embeds ChatGPT directly in a scientific-paper editor, automating drafting and literature triage while raising provenance and citation-verification trade-offs.

posts.title: ChatGPT 5.2 vs Gemini 3.2 Fast: Ars Technica head‑to‑head and what Apple’s Gemini choice means for Siri
posts.excerpt: Ars Technica compares default non‑subscriber models — ChatGPT 5.2 vs Gemini 3.2 Fast — using complex prompts. Read on for test takeaways and how Apple’s Gemini choice affects Siri.

posts.title: How Google DeepMind chose the name 'Nano Banana' — canonical naming note
posts.excerpt: Summarizes Google’s official origin story for the Gemini model name 'Nano Banana', with canonical links, exact phrasing to cite, and practical steps builders should add to docs.

posts.title: Converge Bio raises $25M Series A to scale sequence-trained generative AI for antibody design and protein optimization
posts.excerpt: Converge Bio raised a $25M Series A led by Bessemer to scale sequence-trained generative AI across discovery-to-manufacturing. It already ships antibody-design and protein-yield tools.

posts.title: NVIDIA Rubin and Alpamayo: Six-chip production AI platform and open reasoning models for autonomy
posts.excerpt: At CES 2026 NVIDIA unveiled Rubin - a six-chip production AI platform - and Alpamayo open reasoning models for autonomy, promising roughly 0.1x token costs and OEM demos.

posts.title: Gemma Scope 2 expands open interpretability and reproducible traces across the Gemma 3 family
posts.excerpt: Gemma Scope 2 makes open interpretability tools and reproducible trace exports available across the Gemma 3 family, enabling safety teams to probe and audit complex LLM behavior.

posts.title: Prototyping Multi-node Pretraining and Staged Inference on NVIDIA Hopper and GB200 NVL72
posts.excerpt: A concise playbook to validate multi-node pretraining and staged inference on NVIDIA Hopper and GB200 NVL72 systems. Includes procurement and benchmark checklists and example job specs.

posts.title: DeepMind's FACTS Benchmark Suite: a claim-level framework and quick-start checklist for evaluating LLM factuality
posts.excerpt: DeepMind's FACTS Benchmark Suite evaluates LLM factuality with claim-level tests, error taxonomies and provenance checks. Includes a 5-item quick-start checklist and decision framework.
